text:
below 12) at the time
of conception, when the Moon reaches (due to transit) that
much number of signs from Aries, or when the Moon reaches
(due to transit) that much number of signs, from the sign in
which Moon is placed at conception time, or when the Moon
reaches (due to transit) at the same sign in which Moon is
placed at conception time (in the lOth month of pregnancy)
the birth could take place. All these three possibilities should
be considered. Apart from these three, birth could also take
place when Moon reaches (due to transit) at Navamsa
Dwadasamsa sign of Moon at the time of conception (in the
lOth month of pregnancy), then also birth could take place.
These are all very popular and well-known opinions (of the
sages)]
This Rishi hora sloka, present in ancient astrological classic
Brihat Prajapatya (written by Daksha Prajapati, the student
of Skanda the author of Skanda Hora) formed one of the
foundations for the statement of Mihira, similar to the Saunaka
